saudi_arabia_crude: Yanbu loadings surged to 4.6M bpd (3x 2025 avg) via East-West pipeline but ~30 tankers now within Houthi strike range; Bab al-Mandeb closure threat 'a viable option' per Houthi deputy info minister; US-bound Saudi crude collapsed to ~400k bpd (from >1M avg); Brent $112.78, record 55% monthly surge — CNN, CNBC, Investing.com.
saudi_arabia_lng: Saudi Arabia has no meaningful LNG export capacity; the Jafurah unconventional gas development is in early Phase 1 (450 mmscf/d) and the conflict has underscored the kingdom's strategic urgency to diversify beyond crude oil — Rystad Energy.
saudi_arabia_lpg: Saudi LPG exports curtailed by reduced NGL throughput from offshore field closures; Yanbu bypass partially offsets but pipeline bottleneck (~4.5M bpd port capacity) constrains total volumes — Kpler.
uae_crude: Fujairah crude loadings surged to ~1.9M bpd (up 57% from 2025 avg) as ADNOC maximises Murban output outside Hormuz; ADNOC deferred maintenance May-Sep; IRGC struck Al Taweelah aluminium Mar 29 (6 injured); first post-war Murban availability forecast issued Mar 27 — MEES, World Oil, Al Jazeera.
uae_lng: Das Island LNG (6 Mtpa) at minimal throughput to allow quick restart when Hormuz reopens; Habshan gas complex resumed Mar 23 after 4-day emergency shutdown; ADNOC Gas adjusting LNG/ETL production on transaction-by-transaction basis — Bloomberg, BusinessToday India, S&P Global Platts.
uae_lpg: UAE LPG exports severely disrupted: Shah Gas Field still shut (~1 Bcf/d), Ruwais Refinery-2 offline (major NGL source); Habshan resumed but exports constrained by Hormuz closure — Kpler vessel tracking.
iraq_crude: Iraq crude output plunged to ~1.4M bpd (from 4.3M) with Basra exports near zero; Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline restarted at 250k bpd after Baghdad-KRG deal (first flows in over a decade), but covers only ~6% of lost Basra capacity; FM on all foreign-operated fields since Mar 20 — The National, TRT World, Reuters.
iraq_lng: Iraq has no LNG export infrastructure; associated gas production collapsed alongside 76% crude output reduction, stalling Basra Gas Company projects — Rystad Energy.
iraq_lpg: Iraq's LPG exports effectively halted following FM declaration and shutdown of Basra export terminals; Khor Mor gas processing in Kurdistan also halted — IEA Oil Market Report.
kuwait_crude: Kuwait output plummeted to ~500k bpd as storage fills with Hormuz blocked; KPC declared FM on crude exports; Al-Ahmadi refinery (730 kb/d) struck by Iranian drones Mar 19-20; collective Gulf output drop reached 10M+ bpd by Mar 12 — Fortune, Al Jazeera, IEA Oil Market Report.
kuwait_lng: Kuwait's Al-Zour LNG import FSRU faces acute supply uncertainty as Qatar's LNG entirely offline; Asian spot LNG prices surged past $25/MMBtu; Al-Zour at partial ops for domestic power — Argus Media.
kuwait_lpg: Kuwait's LPG exports disrupted after Al-Ahmadi refinery drone strikes Mar 19-20; NGL extraction from multiple processing units shut; KPC 3-4 months to restore full capacity post-war — Kpler.
iran_crude: Kharg loading ~1.6M bpd (largely unchanged from pre-war); 5 tankers loading Mar 29 (~7.7M bbl); IRGC formalised Hormuz 'toll booth' regime — vessels submit details to approved intermediaries, receive escort codes, tolls settled in yuan; struck UAE/Bahrain aluminium plants Mar 29; 3,500 US troops arrived for potential Kharg raids — Kpler, Fortune, Bloomberg, NBC News.
iran_lng: Iran has no LNG export capacity; Israeli strikes on South Pars Phases 3-6, 14, 17-18 took ~12% of gas output offline; domestic supply severely strained — S&P Global Platts.
iran_lpg: Iran's LPG exports face disruption from Hormuz closure and ~30% shut-in of southern NGL-linked oil fields; IRGC tolled passage charging ~$2M/vessel in yuan; Thailand and Malaysia secured bilateral transit deals — Vortexa, NBC News.
qatar_crude: All Qatar offshore oil fields (~440 kb/d) shut since early March; Laffan Refineries 1 and 2 offline; QatarEnergy FM encompasses all hydrocarbon exports — Bloomberg.
qatar_lng: All 77 Mtpa halted since Mar 2; Trains 4+6 (12.8 Mtpa) sustained 'extensive damage' from Mar 18 missile strikes, $20B/yr lost revenue, 3-5 year repair timeline; FM extended to Edison and Chinese contracts; restart impossible until conflict ends — CNBC, Bloomberg, Reuters, QatarEnergy.
qatar_lpg: Qatar's LPG exports (byproduct of LNG production at Ras Laffan) halted since Mar 2 along with all downstream operations; restart impossible until conflict ends — QatarEnergy statement.
oman_crude: Oman crude surged to ~$121/bbl (premium to Brent ~$101); Mina Al-Fahal briefly evacuated after drone strikes but resumed; Salalah Port struck again Mar 29 (fuel tanks ablaze); inland production ~950 kb/d largely intact; non-Hormuz position gives strategic export advantage — Rigzone, OilPrice.com, Al Jazeera.
oman_lng: Oman LNG's 10.4 Mtpa facility outside Hormuz chokepoint still running; commanding significant Asian spot premiums with Qatar's 77 Mtpa offline — Platts LNG Daily.
oman_lpg: Oman's LPG exports from Fahud and Saih Rawl gas processing largely unaffected; inland production and non-Hormuz export position provide insulation from regional disruption — Kpler.
bahrain_crude: IRGC struck Alba aluminium smelter for third time Mar 29 (2 injured); BAPCO Sitra (405 kb/d) offline since missile strikes Mar 4+9; Al Ma'ameer oil facility also hit with fire and damage; Bapco Energies FM on all operations — Al Jazeera, Al Arabiya, Argus Media.
bahrain_lng: Bahrain depends on Saudi pipeline gas and LNG imports for energy security; Sitra refinery destruction has eliminated domestic refining; regional gas disruption compounds risk — Gulf News.
bahrain_lpg: Bahrain's LPG exports ceased entirely following Sitra refinery destruction and Bapco Energies FM declaration — Argus Media.
russia_crude: Asian imports of Russian fuel oil set for record March; US 30-day sanctions waiver (crude loaded before Mar 12) driving flows; Thailand preparing to open talks on Russian crude purchases; Japan 'considering' Russian crude after US waiver; EU Council President Costa called waiver 'very concerning' — Global Trade Review, Moscow Times, CREA.
russia_lng: Russia's Yamal LNG sent 100% of February exports to EU; Arctic LNG-2 Trains 1-2 operable but below capacity; EU ban on Russian LNG purchases takes effect April 25 — urgewald, ICIS LNG Edge.
russia_lpg: Russia's LPG exports remain a small share of global trade; Gulf conflict has marginally improved Russian NGL competitiveness in Asian markets — Argus Media.
us_crude: SPR exchange: 45.2 mb delivered of 172 mb commitment (120-day schedule from Mar 20); IEA coordinated 400 mb global release; Brent $112.78, WTI $102.88 (record 55% monthly surge); Goldman forecasts $135/bbl if Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb both close (20-25% probability) — DOE, CNBC, Goldman Sachs, Bloomberg.
us_lng: Cheniere now world's largest LNG exporter with Qatar offline; Sabine Pass at 110% nameplate utilisation; all 8 US terminals at max capacity (14.6 Bcf/d total); ~20% global LNG supply gap driving Asian spot up ~39%; capacity to double to ~30 Bcf/d by 2030 — Fortune, EIA, AAF, Global LNG Hub.
us_lpg: US Gulf Coast LPG exports from Enterprise and Targa terminals benefiting from sharply higher Asian demand as Saudi, UAE, Qatari NGL supply curtailed; LPG-naphtha spread favours increased US exports — Argus LPG World.
china_crude: China banned all refined fuel exports Mar 4-5 (except bonded jet/marine fuel, HK/Macau); Sinopec and Zhejiang Petrochemical trimming throughput 10%+; Jan-Feb imports were 11.99M bpd (+15.8% YoY stockpiling); fuel export freeze removed ~$22B/yr swing supply from Asia — Bloomberg, S&P Global, Modern Diplomacy.
china_lng: QatarEnergy extended FM to Chinese LNG contracts Mar 24; Wanhua Chemical FM on Middle East supplies; spot LNG costs surged ~39%; pipeline gas via Power of Siberia provides partial insulation — Reuters.
china_lpg: China (world's largest LPG importer) faces tightening supply as ME NGL-linked LPG from Saudi, UAE, Qatar offline; PDH plant operators scrambling for US and Australian cargoes at sharply higher prices — ICIS.
india_crude: India's crude trade deficit to widen by >$4B in March; Operation Urja Suraksha escorting India-bound tankers (incl. crude tanker Jag Laadki) through Hormuz with frontline warships; Iran granted India 'friendly nation' safe-passage status; India-Russia talks on crude purchases restarted Mar 19 — Maritime Gateway, Business Today, Marine Insight.
india_lng: Qatar supplied 41.4% of India's LNG imports; Petronet LNG declared FM; Gujarat Gas imposed 50% industrial cuts; India negotiating Russian LNG purchases after Puri-Sorokin meeting Mar 19; Petroleum Ministry assured 'sufficient stock' Mar 27 — Business Standard, Organiser, The Trending People.
india_lpg: Indian Navy escorted LPG tankers Pine Gas and Jag Vasant (92,000t combined) through Hormuz under Operation Urja Suraksha; first LPG convoy arrived Indian ports Mar 26-28; ME supply still severely constrained — Business Today, The Week India.
japan_crude: Japan released 80 mb from strategic reserves (largest-ever, 7th in history); oil flowing from 10 national stockpile sites since Mar 16; METI warned additional releases may be needed if disruptions persist past April; ME crude dependence at 93.5%; Japan 'considering' Russian crude after US sanctions waiver — Japan Times, Bloomberg, Moscow Times.
japan_lng: Daiwa Institute warns a 10% ME LNG import cut would push Japan's GDP negative; Asian LNG spot now outpacing European gains by $1-3/MMBtu; Europe losing cargoes as traders divert flexible supply eastward; Japan relying on US spot and Australian contracts — Daiwa Institute, Euronews, Natural Gas Intel.
japan_lpg: Japan's LPG imports for petrochemical and residential heating face tightened supply and surging prices; diversification to US and Australian cargoes underway at significant cost premium — Argus Media.
south_korea_crude: South Korea's 208-day crude reserve provides buffer; SPR release of 22.5 mb (6.0 mb released by Mar 28); naphtha export ban enforced Mar 27; YNCC FM on petrochemical products — Yonhap, ICIS.
south_korea_lng: KOGAS reports inventory exceeds mandatory reserves; Qatar share only ~14% of 2026 imports; Woodside 0.5 Mtpa deal (10.5yr) commenced 2026; TotalEnergies 1 Mtpa deal (10yr) from end-2027; South Korea downplays supply concerns despite Qatar plant damage — Cyprus Mail, KOGAS, S&P Global.
south_korea_lpg: South Korea's LPG imports for naphtha-alternative crackers disrupted by ME supply shutdown; LPG-naphtha price spread shifted as both feedstocks face Gulf-linked constraints — ICIS.
thailand_crude: Thailand secured bilateral Hormuz transit deal with Iran (Mar 28), joining India/Pakistan/Malaysia in 'friendly nation' passage; Deputy PM confirmed Thailand preparing to open talks on purchasing Russian crude to offset ME shortages; Southeast Asia imposing travel limits as oil crisis deepens — Malay Mail, Moscow Times, Al Jazeera.
thailand_lng: Thailand's growing LNG import dependency (declining domestic gas from Gulf of Thailand/Myanmar pipeline) tested as spot LNG prices surged well beyond pre-crisis levels — S&P Global.
thailand_lpg: Thailand's small LPG imports for petrochemical feedstock and cooking fuel face higher prices; relatively low ME LPG dependence limits direct exposure vs larger Asian importers — Argus Media.
vietnam_crude: Nghi Son Refinery turning to US and Qatari crudes as Kuwait supply (~80% of imports) stalls; Decree 72/2026 cut MFN tariffs on petrol/diesel/naphtha to 0% (effective Mar 9, valid through Apr 30); PM Chinh called Kuwaiti PM to reaffirm supply; Vietnam asked Japan/S.Korea for help accessing crude — Argus Media, Nation Thailand, The Diplomat.
vietnam_lng: Vietnam received first 2026 LNG cargo of 63,000 tons at Thi Vai terminal in late Feb; PV GAS confirms supply through April; second FSRU plans at Vung Tau face headwinds from surging spot prices — Vietnam.vn citing PV GAS.
vietnam_lpg: Vietnam's LPG imports (~80% Middle East propane-dependent) face higher procurement costs; small volumes limit exposure relative to major Asian importers — ICIS.
